record.field: FerretDB is an open-source MongoDB-compatible database that proxies the MongoDB wire protocol onto PostgreSQL with DocumentDB, no MongoDB server required.

FerretDB is an open-source MongoDB-compatible database that proxies the MongoDB wire protocol onto PostgreSQL with DocumentDB, no MongoDB server required.

FerretDB acts as a stateless proxy that speaks the MongoDB wire protocol and translates it into queries against a PostgreSQL backend powered by the DocumentDB extension. This lets applications and drivers that expect a MongoDB-style document API keep working while you store the data in a truly open, Apache-2.0-licensed stack. It is aimed at developers and platform teams who want document-database ergonomics without the licensing and lock-in of MongoDB Server, reusing their existing PostgreSQL operational knowledge for backups, monitoring and scaling.

Key features
  • MongoDB wire-protocol compatibility — existing drivers and tools like mongosh connect unchanged
  • PostgreSQL with the DocumentDB extension as a proven, durable storage engine
  • Apache 2.0 open-source license with no per-core or per-node vendor fees
  • Familiar CRUD, aggregation pipelines and indexing over BSON documents
  • Reuse of the PostgreSQL ecosystem for backups, replication and observability
  • Runs as a lightweight internal service, ideal as a shared dependency for other apps
Best for

Teams that need a self-hosted, openly licensed alternative to MongoDB for apps that speak the MongoDB API, and platforms that prefer to consolidate document workloads on their existing PostgreSQL stack. Validate feature compatibility for your specific driver, as FerretDB implements a subset of the MongoDB protocol.

services: ferretdb: image: ferretdb/ferretdb:latest restart: unless-stopped environment: - FERRETDB_POSTGRESQL_URL=postgres://ferretdb:{{ postgres_password }}@postgres:5432/ferretdb depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' expose: - 27017 networks: - internal postgres: image: ghcr.io/ferretdb/postgres-documentdb:latest restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_USER: ferretdb POSTGRES_DB: ferretdb POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' expose: - 5432 networks: - internal healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 5 networks: internal: driver: bridge

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump lógico consistente del backend PostgreSQL/DocumentDB (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura el dump docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía. DocumentDB guarda las colecciones en sus # propios esquemas (no 'public'), por eso contamos todas las tablas de usuario. N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema')\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
Password for the PostgreSQL/DocumentDB backend that stores FerretDB data.
